chars: New app idea. Always on voice recorder and keystroke recorder with active app so you can rebuild your day hour to hour like you said so you can see if you spent 4 hours on a bug.
Keystroke recording: ~200 chars/min fast typist, 8hr day = ~96K chars, ~144K keystrokes, per-event log ~100 bytes = ~14MB/day. Negligible. Voice recording is the expensive part.
Also assuming people are using their own things with Wispr flow or other speech to text apps we actually only need to track the pasted output as keystrokes? Is that possible?

Voice-typed text has distinct fingerprint: variable timing vs robotic uniform timing, irregular bursts vs smooth flow. With enough timing data could label segments as likely voice vs manual. Radical implication: might not need microphone at all.

Ok let's do this. Next is privacy. Thinking local hosted with own api keys offered for free. Or cloud synced and use of automatic model for monthly subscription. What do I need to worry about for hosting this?

The real hard security problems: malware on machine (already owns keystrokes), law enforcement subpoenas (can't protect against), building the service securely. Encryption at rest is real engineering challenge. Key management for BYOK is hard.
I would want to build this as profitable though. What are choices there? Local is a no brainer for dev but want to make sure it's viable before spending real effort on it.

Option 1 Notion model: free local BYOK, Pro $10-15/mo cloud sync + hosted AI. Option 2 hosted AI inference markup. Option 3 one-time purchase. Option 4 B2B teams $8/user/mo. B2B teams is most realistic indie path.
The notion model for sure. And remember MVP is only taking keystrokes and building a daily summary. And then this daily summary is queryable in the future to get how did I fix this bug sort of thing.

8 hours of keystrokes cannot fit in LLM context. Need event compression: raw keystrokes -> sessions -> session summaries -> daily summary. Sessionization and compression is where intelligence lives. Sketched architecture: logger -> SQLite -> LLM pipeline -> query interface.
So hmm the other problem is nowadays we solve by asking an LLM. feel like we will want to capture context around user input too.

Shift: LLM reads content and labels work instead of inferring from window titles. Two levels: context extraction (Phi-3-mini local) every 30-60s, daily summary (large model) once per day. Storage gets richer with context_labels table.
The context is easy. We would feed hour by hour with some overlap.
Revised architecture: logger writes continuously to SQLite, end of day LLM pipeline processes hour-by-hour with 15min overlap, query interface. Simpler, same output, no real-time inference.
